name: S3 Huahin Hotel
detail: Probably the most comfortable bed I slept in.
area: Hua Hin City Center,Hua Hin / Cha-am - View on map

name: RELO' The Urban Escape
detail: The facility, pool, beach and atmosphere are undeniable.
area: Hua Hin Beachfront,Hua Hin / Cha-am - View on map

name: The Yana Villas Hua Hin
detail: The Yana Villas located in Khao Takiab, a place which quite peaceful and quiet that 6km far from the crowded Hua Hin center city. The villa is simple (don't expect it like a 5-star hotel), and located just right beside the beach, you can watch the beautiful sunset (like the display photo) beside the pool. Eating breakfast beside the pool, enjoy the wind blows (beware the strong winds might blow all the tissue papers away). The breakfast is excellent, and huge amount for me. Their staffs are friendly and guided us well. Even they don't speak fluent English, but their smiley faces made you felt warm. The hotel provided their own shower gels, shampoo and conditioner, which is all in lemon grass series. For a lemon grass lover like me, you can really smell that everywhere. The only bathtub i felt, it shall included the anti-slip texture, to avoid any slippery.
area: Khao Takiab Beach Front,Hua Hin / Cha-am - View on map

name: City Beach Resort
detail: Though we live in Hua Hin we stay here regularly if we're attending an event, this time it was the local Jazz Festival on the beach. The hotel is large, as are the rooms, and whilst it's comfortable the rooms are very tired, with wallpaper peeling off the walls. As we booked at the last moment we booked via Agoda and paid on arrival, I was a little concerned that after my card had been processed and payment authorised, the lady on reception copied my card details onto a peice of paper, I thought the numbers were blocked out for a reason, the security of the account, she was interested in my concerns. Check-out, as always is painfully slow whilst you have to wait whilst they get someone to check your room. The hotel is ok for the odd night, but probably not for an extended stay, though we would certainly stay there again.
area: Hua Hin City Center,Hua Hin / Cha-am - View on map

name: The Standard Hua Hin
detail: The pool area is exceptional and relaxing with ocean view.
area: Hua Hin City Center,Hua Hin / Cha-am - View on map

name: ibis Hua Hin
detail: Great convient hotel in Hua Hin. Rooms are small, but they are clean and modern and the beds are the most comfortable ones I've slept in. We chose this hotel because it was next to a friends condo. Access to the beach is good and there is a great night market 400 m from the hotel. Best thing was that we were upgraded to a family room, so a little more space and bunk beds for our little guy which he made into an extra playing area. The pool isn't the greatest, but good enough to get wet. Will definitely stay here again.
area: Khao Takiab,Hua Hin / Cha-am - View on map

name: Holiday Inn Resort Vana Nava Hua Hin By IHG
detail: You can enjoy in buffet breakfast They have their own shuttle bus around the Hua hin and the hotel ( so you will save your money) They have Free entrance to the best waterpark in Hua Hin They have the best reservation and administration team . They are kindly and friendly I am really enjoy in my stay and I classify it as one of my favorite stay in my travel.
area: Khao Takiab,Hua Hin / Cha-am - View on map

name: Kiang Haad Beach Hua Hin Hotel
detail: We stayed for 3 days and 2 nights at this hotel. The hotel staff was very kind and friendly. The location is very strategic. It is walking distance to Hua Hin Village Mall, bus station and 2-3- minute walk to the beach. 100 meters to 7/11 store. The breakfast was quite ok. The room was clean but a little bit small for 3 of us. You could also hear the noise of people outside. The hotel offers car rental and that’s great if ever you want to go the other spots in Hua Hin. The WiFi was not that strong on the 5th floor where we stayed. I turned it off and used my 4G. Moreover, I will recommend this hotel to my friends and maybe plan to stay here again soon.
area: Hua Hin City Center,Hua Hin / Cha-am - View on map

name: Hua Hin Loft Managed by Loft Group
detail: When I first checked in I was not given the mountain-view room I had booked, but after complaining I was moved to the correct room. The room was very large and very clean. The fridge was full of drinks for reasonable prices. The mountain view from the 4th floor was phenomenal. The road the hotel on is a very nice and quiet side street connected to the main road where the baht buses frequently run up & down (10 baht per ride). It's a short walk to the beach and the hotel is situated near a crosswalk which makes crossing the busy main road somewhat easier. The hotel's lobby and entrance is very nicely decorated. I searched agoda.com for a very long time to find the right hotel in Hua Hin, and I am glad I eventually found this place. *Key note: If you stay there, you will likely run into a few stray dogs who hang out on the front steps. BE SURE TO STOP TO SPEND A FEW MINUTES PETTING THEM. THEY REALLY APPRECIATE IT. ESPECIALLY THE OVERWEIGHT TAN DOG.
area: Hua Hin City Center,Hua Hin / Cha-am - View on map

name: Radisson Resort & Spa Hua Hin
detail: We were looking for a nice quiet location to recover from a very busy holiday period - and we found the perfect place with the Novotel Hua Hin Cha-Am Resort & Spa. First off, the property is located on the much quieter Cha-Am section. Next, all rooms face the Gulf so beautiful views of sunrise and moonrises. Next, the staff is very friendly and helpful. There's a very large pool and deck area that allows you to either be close to the pool action or a bit further away - including a sandy beach area overlooking the Gulf (there is, though, really no beach here - but that did not detract fron the property at all). Just a short walk outside the property are a number of small restaurants, bars, and shops that are very typical for Thailand. There are four restaurants on property (and a wonderful little wine bar on the roof - fantastic views). The only down side is that the beach side restaurant has a very limited menu that leaves just a little bit to be desired for that truly romantic dinner - that and a number of mosquitoes that joined us for dinner. The only other down side item (and this is being a bit picky) were the towels - a bit worn and frayed. Other than those two items, we highly recommend this property and WILL be heading back when we once again need a break from the rat race here in the city.
area: Cha Am Beachfront,Hua Hin / Cha-am - View on map
